Appendix A – Course Codes

Course Codes are found in the Course Codes – App A spreadsheet on the Pennsylvania Department of Education > Data and Reporting > PIMS > Manuals and Calendar page.

Each course code submitted to PIMS must be mapped to a standardized state course code; the Pennsylvania Department of Education (PDE) lists standardized courses for use in federal reporting for Act 13 Building Score calculation, the Future Ready PA Index, and ESSA.

The standardized course list is based on the 5-digit course codes in the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) School Codes for the Exchange of Data (SCED) classification system for courses in the United States. Information, including course descriptions, is available in the School Course Classification System: School Codes for Exchange of Data (SCED), found on the NCES website (nces.ed.gov/forum/SCED.asp).

Beginning in the 2019-20 school year, LEAs are required to submit all course subject areas. The new subject area data is required to support new federal reporting requirements that are not limited to the Core Content subjects. Only Core Content subject area courses will still be used to calculate the Building Score calculation used in educator evaluation, as prescribed under ACT 13 of 2020 (Note: School-level PVAAS is also included in the Act 13 Building Score calculation but is not indicated in this appendix).

SY 22-23 STATE COURSE CODESCED CODESY 22-23 STATE COURSE NAMESUBJECT AREASCED DESCRIPTIONAPIBDUAL CREDITFUTURE READY
0100101001English/Language Arts I (9th grade)English/Language ArtsEnglish/Language Arts I (9th grade) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing and usually include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Typically, these courses introduce and define various genres of literature, with writing exercises often linked to reading selections.
E0100101001English/Language Arts I (9th grade) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: English/Language Arts I (9th grade) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing and usually include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Typically, these courses introduce and define various genres of literature, with writing exercises often linked to reading selections.
H0100101001English/Language Arts I (9th grade) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: English/Language Arts I (9th grade) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing and usually include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Typically, these courses introduce and define various genres of literature, with writing exercises often linked to reading selections.
S0100101001English/Language Arts I (9th grade) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: English/Language Arts I (9th grade) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing and usually include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Typically, these courses introduce and define various genres of literature, with writing exercises often linked to reading selections.
V0100101001English/Language Arts I (9th grade) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: English/Language Arts I (9th grade) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing and usually include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Typically, these courses introduce and define various genres of literature, with writing exercises often linked to reading selections.
0100201002English/Language Arts II (10th grade)English/Language ArtsEnglish/Language Arts II (10th grade) courses usually offer a balanced focus on composition and literature. Typically, students learn about the alternate aims and audiences of written compositions by writing persuasive, critical, and creative multi-paragraph essays and compositions. Through the study of various genres of literature, students can improve their reading rate and comprehension and develop the skills to determine the author’s intent and theme and to recognize the techniques used by the author to deliver his or her message.
E0100201002English/Language Arts II (10th grade) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: English/Language Arts II (10th grade) courses usually offer a balanced focus on composition and literature. Typically, students learn about the alternate aims and audiences of written compositions by writing persuasive, critical, and creative multi-paragraph essays and compositions. Through the study of various genres of literature, students can improve their reading rate and comprehension and develop the skills to determine the author’s intent and theme and to recognize the techniques used by the author to deliver his or her message.
H0100201002English/Language Arts II (10th grade) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: English/Language Arts II (10th grade) courses usually offer a balanced focus on composition and literature. Typically, students learn about the alternate aims and audiences of written compositions by writing persuasive, critical, and creative multi-paragraph essays and compositions. Through the study of various genres of literature, students can improve their reading rate and comprehension and develop the skills to determine the author’s intent and theme and to recognize the techniques used by the author to deliver his or her message.
S0100201002English/Language Arts II (10th grade) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: English/Language Arts II (10th grade) courses usually offer a balanced focus on composition and literature. Typically, students learn about the alternate aims and audiences of written compositions by writing persuasive, critical, and creative multi-paragraph essays and compositions. Through the study of various genres of literature, students can improve their reading rate and comprehension and develop the skills to determine the author’s intent and theme and to recognize the techniques used by the author to deliver his or her message.
V0100201002English/Language Arts II (10th grade) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: English/Language Arts II (10th grade) courses usually offer a balanced focus on composition and literature. Typically, students learn about the alternate aims and audiences of written compositions by writing persuasive, critical, and creative multi-paragraph essays and compositions. Through the study of various genres of literature, students can improve their reading rate and comprehension and develop the skills to determine the author’s intent and theme and to recognize the techniques used by the author to deliver his or her message.
0100301003English/Language Arts III (11th grade)English/Language ArtsEnglish/Language Arts III (11th grade) courses continue to develop students’ writing skills, emphasizing clear, logical writing patterns, word choice, and usage, as students write essays and begin to learn the techniques of writing research papers. Students continue to read works of literature, which often form the backbone of the writing assignments. Literary conventions and stylistic devices may receive greater emphasis than in previous courses.
E0100301003English/Language Arts III (11th grade) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: English/Language Arts III (11th grade) courses continue to develop students’ writing skills, emphasizing clear, logical writing patterns, word choice, and usage, as students write essays and begin to learn the techniques of writing research papers. Students continue to read works of literature, which often form the backbone of the writing assignments. Literary conventions and stylistic devices may receive greater emphasis than in previous courses.
H0100301003English/Language Arts III (11th grade) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: English/Language Arts III (11th grade) courses continue to develop students’ writing skills, emphasizing clear, logical writing patterns, word choice, and usage, as students write essays and begin to learn the techniques of writing research papers. Students continue to read works of literature, which often form the backbone of the writing assignments. Literary conventions and stylistic devices may receive greater emphasis than in previous courses.
S0100301003English/Language Arts III (11th grade) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: English/Language Arts III (11th grade) courses continue to develop students’ writing skills, emphasizing clear, logical writing patterns, word choice, and usage, as students write essays and begin to learn the techniques of writing research papers. Students continue to read works of literature, which often form the backbone of the writing assignments. Literary conventions and stylistic devices may receive greater emphasis than in previous courses.
V0100301003English/Language Arts III (11th grade) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: English/Language Arts III (11th grade) courses continue to develop students’ writing skills, emphasizing clear, logical writing patterns, word choice, and usage, as students write essays and begin to learn the techniques of writing research papers. Students continue to read works of literature, which often form the backbone of the writing assignments. Literary conventions and stylistic devices may receive greater emphasis than in previous courses.
0100401004English/Language Arts IV (12th grade)English/Language ArtsEnglish/Language Arts IV (12th grade) courses blend composition and literature into a cohesive whole as students write critical and comparative analyses of selected literature, continuing to develop their language arts skills. Typically, students primarily write multi-paragraph essays, but they may also write one or more major research papers.
E0100401004English/Language Arts IV (12th grade) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: English/Language Arts IV (12th grade) courses blend composition and literature into a cohesive whole as students write critical and comparative analyses of selected literature, continuing to develop their language arts skills. Typically, students primarily write multi-paragraph essays, but they may also write one or more major research papers.
H0100401004English/Language Arts IV (12th grade) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: English/Language Arts IV (12th grade) courses blend composition and literature into a cohesive whole as students write critical and comparative analyses of selected literature, continuing to develop their language arts skills. Typically, students primarily write multi-paragraph essays, but they may also write one or more major research papers.
S0100401004English/Language Arts IV (12th grade) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: English/Language Arts IV (12th grade) courses blend composition and literature into a cohesive whole as students write critical and comparative analyses of selected literature, continuing to develop their language arts skills. Typically, students primarily write multi-paragraph essays, but they may also write one or more major research papers.
V0100401004English/Language Arts IV (12th grade) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: English/Language Arts IV (12th grade) courses blend composition and literature into a cohesive whole as students write critical and comparative analyses of selected literature, continuing to develop their language arts skills. Typically, students primarily write multi-paragraph essays, but they may also write one or more major research papers.
0100501005AP English Language and CompositionEnglish/Language ArtsFollowing the College Board’s suggested curriculum designed to parallel college-level English courses, AP English Language and Composition courses expose students to prose written in a variety of periods, disciplines, and rhetorical contexts. These courses emphasize the interaction of authorial purpose, intended audience, and the subject at hand, and through them, students learn to develop stylistic flexibility as they write compositions covering a variety of subjects that are intended for various purposes.YY
0100601006AP English Literature and CompositionEnglish/Language ArtsFollowing the College Board’s suggested curriculum designed to parallel college-level English courses, AP English Literature and Composition courses enable students to develop critical standards for evaluating literature. Students study the language, character, action, and theme in works of recognized literary merit; enrich their understanding of connotation, metaphor, irony, syntax, and tone; and write compositions of their own (including literary analysis, exposition, argument, narrative, and creative writing).YY
0100701007IB Language A: Literature—EnglishEnglish/Language ArtsIB Language A: Literature—English courses prepare students to take the International Baccalaureate Language A: Literature exams. Course content includes in-depth study of a range of literary texts in a number of literary forms and from different times and places, written analyses and critiques of this literature, and other oral and written assignments. The course is designed for students highly competent in using English in an academic context.YY
0100801008English as a Second LanguageEnglish/Language ArtsEnglish as a Second Language (ESL) courses are designed for the acquisition and rapid mastery of the English language, focusing on reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills. ESL courses usually begin with extensive listening and speaking practice, building on auditory and oral skills, and then move on to reading and writing. These courses provide an explanation of basic structures of the English language, enabling students to progress from an elementary understanding of English words and verb tenses to a more comprehensive grasp of various formal and informal styles and then to advance to “regular” English courses. ESL classes may also include an orientation to the customs and culture of the diverse population in the United States.
0100901009Language Arts LaboratoryEnglish/Language ArtsLanguage Arts Laboratory courses provide instruction in basic language skills, integrating reading, writing, speaking, and listening, while placing great emphasis on the progress of individual students. Course content depends upon students’ abilities and may include vocabulary building, improving spelling and grammar, developing writing and composition skills, reading silently or aloud, and improving listening and comprehension abilities.
0101001010IB Language A (English), Middle Years ProgramEnglish/Language ArtsInternational Baccalaureate (IB) Language A (English), Middle Years Program courses include instruction in the instrumental function of a language, emphasizing skills in listening, viewing, speaking, reading, presenting, and writing, as well as literature encompassing a variety of periods and genres.YY
0101101011IB Language A: Language and Literature—EnglishEnglish/Language ArtsIB Language A: Language and Literature—English courses prepare students to take the International Baccalaureate Language A: Language and Literature exams. Course content includes in-depth study of various text types and literary forms, composition for purpose and audience, and the context and elements of literature. The course includes oral and written assignments and is designed for students highly competent in using English in an academic context.YY
0101201012IB Literature and PerformanceEnglish/Language ArtsIB Literature and Performance courses prepare students to take the International Baccalaureate Literature and Performance exam. Course content focuses on the relationship between literature and theatre. These courses may cover the critical study of literary texts, the exploration of chosen approaches to a text, and the performance of written texts.YY
0101301013AP Seminar: EnglishEnglish/Language ArtsFollowing the College Board’s suggested curriculum designed to parallel college-level English courses, AP Seminar: English courses expose students to a variety of texts covering multiple genres, topics, and rhetorical contexts in a seminar-style setting. These courses foster students’ ability to summarize and explain the salient ideas in a text by analyzing an author’s perspective, rhetorical choices, and argumentative structure. Students evaluate a variety of literary, informational, and visual texts, and synthesize perspectives to develop evidence-based arguments. Students convey their findings through multiple written formats, multimedia presentations, and oral defenses.YY
0101401014CI English LanguageEnglish/Language ArtsCambridge International (CI) English Language courses prepare students to take the Cambridge English Language assessments. These courses teach students to critically respond to a variety of texts in a range of forms, styles, and contexts, and promote skills of communication, reading, research, and analysis. Students will gain an understanding of English language features and issues as well we develop writing skills for different purposes and audiences.
0102601026Language Arts (early childhood education)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (early childhood education) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts learning in reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for early childhood education.
E0102601026Language Arts (early childhood education) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (early childhood education) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts learning in reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for early childhood education.
H0102601026Language Arts (early childhood education) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (early childhood education) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts learning in reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for early childhood education.
S0102601026Language Arts (early childhood education) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (early childhood education) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts learning in reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for early childhood education.
V0102601026Language Arts (early childhood education) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (early childhood education) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts learning in reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for early childhood education.
0102701027Language Arts (pre-kindergarten)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (pre-kindergarten) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts learning in reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for pre-kindergarten education.
E0102701027Language Arts (pre-kindergarten) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (pre-kindergarten) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts learning in reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for pre-kindergarten education.
H0102701027Language Arts (pre-kindergarten) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (pre-kindergarten) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts learning in reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for pre-kindergarten education.
S0102701027Language Arts (pre-kindergarten) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (pre-kindergarten) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts learning in reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for pre-kindergarten education.
V0102701027Language Arts (pre-kindergarten) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (pre-kindergarten) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts learning in reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for pre-kindergarten education.
0102801028Language Arts (kindergarten)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (kindergarten) courses engage students in activities to develop their language arts skills (reading, writing, listening, and speaking). Course content may emphasize storytelling or reading aloud and evoking a written, oral, or pictorial response. Specific content depends upon state standards for kindergarten.
E0102801028Language Arts (kindergarten) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (kindergarten) courses engage students in activities to develop their language arts skills (reading, writing, listening, and speaking). Course content may emphasize storytelling or reading aloud and evoking a written, oral, or pictorial response. Specific content depends upon state standards for kindergarten.
H0102801028Language Arts (kindergarten) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (kindergarten) courses engage students in activities to develop their language arts skills (reading, writing, listening, and speaking). Course content may emphasize storytelling or reading aloud and evoking a written, oral, or pictorial response. Specific content depends upon state standards for kindergarten.
S0102801028Language Arts (kindergarten) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (kindergarten) courses engage students in activities to develop their language arts skills (reading, writing, listening, and speaking). Course content may emphasize storytelling or reading aloud and evoking a written, oral, or pictorial response. Specific content depends upon state standards for kindergarten.
V0102801028Language Arts (kindergarten) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (kindergarten) courses engage students in activities to develop their language arts skills (reading, writing, listening, and speaking). Course content may emphasize storytelling or reading aloud and evoking a written, oral, or pictorial response. Specific content depends upon state standards for kindergarten.
0102901029Language Arts (grade 1)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (grade 1) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize recognition of and response to various types of text, extension of vocabulary and writing skills, and the connection of language to the expression of ideas. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 1.
E0102901029Language Arts (grade 1) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (grade 1) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize recognition of and response to various types of text, extension of vocabulary and writing skills, and the connection of language to the expression of ideas. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 1.
H0102901029Language Arts (grade 1) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (grade 1) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize recognition of and response to various types of text, extension of vocabulary and writing skills, and the connection of language to the expression of ideas. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 1.
S0102901029Language Arts (grade 1) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (grade 1) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize recognition of and response to various types of text, extension of vocabulary and writing skills, and the connection of language to the expression of ideas. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 1.
V0102901029Language Arts (grade 1) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (grade 1) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize recognition of and response to various types of text, extension of vocabulary and writing skills, and the connection of language to the expression of ideas. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 1.
0103001030Language Arts (grade 2)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (grade 2) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may build students’ skills in independent reading and writing by increasing reading and writing fluency, vocabulary, and recognition of word and language patterns. They may also introduce or reinforce the rules of grammar. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 2.
E0103001030Language Arts (grade 2) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (grade 2) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may build students’ skills in independent reading and writing by increasing reading and writing fluency, vocabulary, and recognition of word and language patterns. They may also introduce or reinforce the rules of grammar. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 2.
H0103001030Language Arts (grade 2) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (grade 2) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may build students’ skills in independent reading and writing by increasing reading and writing fluency, vocabulary, and recognition of word and language patterns. They may also introduce or reinforce the rules of grammar. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 2.
S0103001030Language Arts (grade 2) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (grade 2) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may build students␀␀’ skills in independent reading and writing by increasing reading and writing fluency, vocabulary, and recognition of word and language patterns. They may also introduce or reinforce the rules of grammar. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 2.
V0103001030Language Arts (grade 2) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (grade 2) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may build students’ skills in independent reading and writing by increasing reading and writing fluency, vocabulary, and recognition of word and language patterns. They may also introduce or reinforce the rules of grammar. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 2.
0103101031Language Arts (grade 3)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (grade 3) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize independent reading and writing in a variety of assignments. Some emphasis may also be placed on presentation or oral communication skills. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 3.
E0103101031Language Arts (grade 3) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (grade 3) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize independent reading and writing in a variety of assignments. Some emphasis may also be placed on presentation or oral communication skills. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 3.
H0103101031Language Arts (grade 3) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (grade 3) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize independent reading and writing in a variety of assignments. Some emphasis may also be placed on presentation or oral communication skills. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 3.
S0103101031Language Arts (grade 3) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (grade 3) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize independent reading and writing in a variety of assignments. Some emphasis may also be placed on presentation or oral communication skills. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 3.
V0103101031Language Arts (grade 3) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (grade 3) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize independent reading and writing in a variety of assignments. Some emphasis may also be placed on presentation or oral communication skills. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 3.
0103201032Language Arts (grade 4)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (grade 4) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize independent reading and writing in a variety of assignments and require students to respond to different material in different ways. Grammar and usage of the English language may also be emphasized. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 4.
E0103201032Language Arts (grade 4) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (grade 4) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize independent reading and writing in a variety of assignments and require students to respond to different material in different ways. Grammar and usage of the English language may also be emphasized. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 4.
H0103201032Language Arts (grade 4) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (grade 4) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize independent reading and writing in a variety of assignments and require students to respond to different material in different ways. Grammar and usage of the English language may also be emphasized. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 4.
S0103201032Language Arts (grade 4) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (grade 4) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize independent reading and writing in a variety of assignments and require students to respond to different material in different ways. Grammar and usage of the English language may also be emphasized. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 4.
V0103201032Language Arts (grade 4) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (grade 4) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize independent reading and writing in a variety of assignments and require students to respond to different material in different ways. Grammar and usage of the English language may also be emphasized. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 4.
0103301033Language Arts (grade 5)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (grade 5) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may extend students’ skills in composition, writing for particular purposes or audiences, distinguishing meaning and literary functions in texts, and fluency in reading. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 5.
E0103301033Language Arts (grade 5) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (grade 5) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may extend students’ skills in composition, writing for particular purposes or audiences, distinguishing meaning and literary functions in texts, and fluency in reading. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 5.
H0103301033Language Arts (grade 5) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (grade 5) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may extend students’ skills in composition, writing for particular purposes or audiences, distinguishing meaning and literary functions in texts, and fluency in reading. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 5.
S0103301033Language Arts (grade 5) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (grade 5) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may extend students’ skills in composition, writing for particular purposes or audiences, distinguishing meaning and literary functions in texts, and fluency in reading. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 5.
V0103301033Language Arts (grade 5) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (grade 5) courses include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may extend students’ skills in composition, writing for particular purposes or audiences, distinguishing meaning and literary functions in texts, and fluency in reading. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 5.
0103401034Language Arts (grade 6)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (grade 6) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 6.
E0103401034Language Arts (grade 6) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (grade 6) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 6.
H0103401034Language Arts (grade 6) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (grade 6) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 6.
S0103401034Language Arts (grade 6) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (grade 6) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 6.
V0103401034Language Arts (grade 6) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (grade 6) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. These courses may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 6.
0103501035Language Arts (grade 7)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (grade 7) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Beyond emphasizing different uses for language, these courses may also include using language (particularly written text) to construct meaning and connections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 7.
E0103501035Language Arts (grade 7) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (grade 7) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Beyond emphasizing different uses for language, these courses may also include using language (particularly written text) to construct meaning and connections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 7.
H0103501035Language Arts (grade 7) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (grade 7) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Beyond emphasizing different uses for language, these courses may also include using language (particularly written text) to construct meaning and connections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 7.
S0103501035Language Arts (grade 7) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (grade 7) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Beyond emphasizing different uses for language, these courses may also include using language (particularly written text) to construct meaning and connections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 7.
V0103501035Language Arts (grade 7) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (grade 7) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Beyond emphasizing different uses for language, these courses may also include using language (particularly written text) to construct meaning and connections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 7.
0103601036Language Arts (grade 8)English/Language ArtsLanguage Arts (grade 8) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Typically, these courses use various genres of literature to improve reading skills, and they link writing exercises for different purposes to those reading selections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 8.
E0103601036Language Arts (grade 8) - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts (grade 8) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Typically, these courses use various genres of literature to improve reading skills, and they link writing exercises for different purposes to those reading selections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 8.
H0103601036Language Arts (grade 8) - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts (grade 8) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Typically, these courses use various genres of literature to improve reading skills, and they link writing exercises for different purposes to those reading selections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 8.
S0103601036Language Arts (grade 8) - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts (grade 8) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Typically, these courses use various genres of literature to improve reading skills, and they link writing exercises for different purposes to those reading selections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 8.
V0103601036Language Arts (grade 8) - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts (grade 8) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge of grammar, vocabulary, word usage, and the mechanics of writing, and include the four aspects of language use: reading, writing, speaking, and listening. Typically, these courses use various genres of literature to improve reading skills, and they link writing exercises for different purposes to those reading selections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 8.
0103701037Language Arts—GeneralEnglish/Language ArtsLanguage Arts—General courses involve content that is not grade differentiated and may apply to a range of consecutive grades (e.g., "by third grade, students should know and be able to do…"). These courses include content that may be applicable to states or localities that do not employ grade-level content standards. [These courses provide broad content that is not organized as described elsewhere (i.e., Language Arts, early childhood education through grade 8).]
E0103701037Language Arts—General - English LearnerEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to English Learners: Language Arts—General courses involve content that is not grade differentiated and may apply to a range of consecutive grades (e.g., "by third grade, students should know and be able to do…"). These courses include content that may be applicable to states or localities that do not employ grade-level content standards. [These courses provide broad content that is not organized as described elsewhere (i.e., Language Arts, early childhood education through grade 8).]
H0103701037Language Arts—General - Hearing ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Language Arts—General courses involve content that is not grade differentiated and may apply to a range of consecutive grades (e.g., "by third grade, students should know and be able to do…"). These courses include content that may be applicable to states or localities that do not employ grade-level content standards. [These courses provide broad content that is not organized as described elsewhere (i.e., Language Arts, early childhood education through grade 8).]
S0103701037Language Arts—General - Special EducationEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Special Education: Language Arts—General courses involve content that is not grade differentiated and may apply to a range of consecutive grades (e.g., "by third grade, students should know and be able to do…"). These courses include content that may be applicable to states or localities that do not employ grade-level content standards. [These courses provide broad content that is not organized as described elsewhere (i.e., Language Arts, early childhood education through grade 8).]
V0103701037Language Arts—General - Visually ImpairedEnglish/Language ArtsSpecific to Visually Impaired: Language Arts—General courses involve content that is not grade differentiated and may apply to a range of consecutive grades (e.g., "by third grade, students should know and be able to do…"). These courses include content that may be applicable to states or localities that do not employ grade-level content standards. [These courses provide broad content that is not organized as described elsewhere (i.e., Language Arts, early childhood education through grade 8).]
0103801038Reading (early childhood education)ReadingReading (early childhood education) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts, particularly focusing on reading skills. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age-appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for early childhood education.
E0103801038Reading (early childhood education) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (early childhood education) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts, particularly focusing on reading skills. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age-appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for early childhood education.
H0103801038Reading (early childhood education) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (early childhood education) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts, particularly focusing on reading skills. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age-appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for early childhood education.
S0103801038Reading (early childhood education) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (early childhood education) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts, particularly focusing on reading skills. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age-appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for early childhood education.
V0103801038Reading (early childhood education) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (early childhood education) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts, particularly focusing on reading skills. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age-appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for early childhood education.
0103901039Reading (pre-kindergarten)ReadingReading (pre-kindergarten) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts, particularly focusing on reading skills. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age-appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for pre-kindergarten education.
E0103901039Reading (pre-kindergarten) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (pre-kindergarten) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts, particularly focusing on reading skills. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age-appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for pre-kindergarten education.
H0103901039Reading (pre-kindergarten) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (pre-kindergarten) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts, particularly focusing on reading skills. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age-appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for pre-kindergarten education.
S0103901039Reading (pre-kindergarten) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (pre-kindergarten) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts, particularly focusing on reading skills. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age-appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for pre-kindergarten education.
V0103901039Reading (pre-kindergarten) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (pre-kindergarten) courses develop students’ readiness for language arts, particularly focusing on reading skills. Courses may include activities related to phonics, phonemic awareness, and vocabulary. Content is age-appropriate and conforms to any existing state standards for pre-kindergarten education.
0104001040Reading (kindergarten)ReadingReading (kindergarten) courses engage students in activities to develop their reading skills. Course content may emphasize storytelling or reading aloud and evoking a written, oral, or pictorial response. Specific content depends upon state standards for kindergarten.
E0104001040Reading (kindergarten) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (kindergarten) courses engage students in activities to develop their reading skills. Course content may emphasize storytelling or reading aloud and evoking a written, oral, or pictorial response. Specific content depends upon state standards for kindergarten.
H0104001040Reading (kindergarten) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (kindergarten) courses engage students in activities to develop their reading skills. Course content may emphasize storytelling or reading aloud and evoking a written, oral, or pictorial response. Specific content depends upon state standards for kindergarten.
S0104001040Reading (kindergarten) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (kindergarten) courses engage students in activities to develop their reading skills. Course content may emphasize storytelling or reading aloud and evoking a written, oral, or pictorial response. Specific content depends upon state standards for kindergarten.
V0104001040Reading (kindergarten) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (kindergarten) courses engage students in activities to develop their reading skills. Course content may emphasize storytelling or reading aloud and evoking a written, oral, or pictorial response. Specific content depends upon state standards for kindergarten.
0104101041Reading (grade 1)ReadingReading (grade 1) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize recognition of and response to various types of text; extension of vocabulary; and the connection of language to the expression of ideas. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 1.
E0104101041Reading (grade 1) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (grade 1) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize recognition of and response to various types of text; extension of vocabulary; and the connection of language to the expression of ideas. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 1.
H0104101041Reading (grade 1) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (grade 1) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize recognition of and response to various types of text; extension of vocabulary; and the connection of language to the expression of ideas. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 1.
S0104101041Reading (grade 1) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (grade 1) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize recognition of and response to various types of text; extension of vocabulary; and the connection of language to the expression of ideas. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 1.
V0104101041Reading (grade 1) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (grade 1) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize recognition of and response to various types of text; extension of vocabulary; and the connection of language to the expression of ideas. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 1.
0104201042Reading (grade 2)ReadingReading (grade 2) courses focus on reading skills and may build students’ skills in independent reading by increasing reading fluency, vocabulary, and recognition of word and language patterns. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 2.
E0104201042Reading (grade 2) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (grade 2) courses focus on reading skills and may build students’ skills in independent reading by increasing reading fluency, vocabulary, and recognition of word and language patterns. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 2.
H0104201042Reading (grade 2) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (grade 2) courses focus on reading skills and may build students’ skills in independent reading by increasing reading fluency, vocabulary, and recognition of word and language patterns. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 2.
S0104201042Reading (grade 2) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (grade 2) courses focus on reading skills and may build students’ skills in independent reading by increasing reading fluency, vocabulary, and recognition of word and language patterns. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 2.
V0104201042Reading (grade 2) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (grade 2) courses focus on reading skills and may build students’ skills in independent reading by increasing reading fluency, vocabulary, and recognition of word and language patterns. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 2.
0104301043Reading (grade 3)ReadingReading (grade 3) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize independent reading in a variety of assignments. Some emphasis may also be placed on presentation skills. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 3.
E0104301043Reading (grade 3) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (grade 3) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize independent reading in a variety of assignments. Some emphasis may also be placed on presentation skills. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 3.
H0104301043Reading (grade 3) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (grade 3) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize independent reading in a variety of assignments. Some emphasis may also be placed on presentation skills. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 3.
S0104301043Reading (grade 3) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (grade 3) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize independent reading in a variety of assignments. Some emphasis may also be placed on presentation skills. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 3.
V0104301043Reading (grade 3) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (grade 3) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize independent reading in a variety of assignments. Some emphasis may also be placed on presentation skills. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 3.
0104401044Reading (grade 4)ReadingReading (grade 4) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize independent reading in a variety of assignments and require students to respond to different material in different ways. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 4.
E0104401044Reading (grade 4) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (grade 4) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize independent reading in a variety of assignments and require students to respond to different material in different ways. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 4.
H0104401044Reading (grade 4) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (grade 4) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize independent reading in a variety of assignments and require students to respond to different material in different ways. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 4.
S0104401044Reading (grade 4) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (grade 4) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize independent reading in a variety of assignments and require students to respond to different material in different ways. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 4.
V0104401044Reading (grade 4) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (grade 4) courses focus on reading skills and may emphasize independent reading in a variety of assignments and require students to respond to different material in different ways. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 4.
0104501045Reading (grade 5)ReadingReading (grade 5) courses focus on reading skills and may extend students’ skills in distinguishing meaning and literary functions in texts, and fluency in reading. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 5.
E0104501045Reading (grade 5) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (grade 5) courses focus on reading skills and may extend students’ skills in distinguishing meaning and literary functions in texts, and fluency in reading. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 5.
H0104501045Reading (grade 5) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (grade 5) courses focus on reading skills and may extend students’ skills in distinguishing meaning and literary functions in texts, and fluency in reading. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 5.
S0104501045Reading (grade 5) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (grade 5) courses focus on reading skills and may extend students’ skills in distinguishing meaning and literary functions in texts, and fluency in reading. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 5.
V0104501045Reading (grade 5) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (grade 5) courses focus on reading skills and may extend students’ skills in distinguishing meaning and literary functions in texts, and fluency in reading. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 5.
0104601046Reading (grade 6)ReadingReading (grade 6) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading and may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 6.
E0104601046Reading (grade 6) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (grade 6) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading and may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 6.
H0104601046Reading (grade 6) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (grade 6) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading and may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 6.
S0104601046Reading (grade 6) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (grade 6) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading and may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 6.
V0104601046Reading (grade 6) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (grade 6) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading and may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 6.
0104701047Reading (grade 7)ReadingReading (grade 7) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading and may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 7.
E0104701047Reading (grade 7) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (grade 7) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading and may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 7.
H0104701047Reading (grade 7) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (grade 7) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading and may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 7.
S0104701047Reading (grade 7) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (grade 7) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading and may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 7.
V0104701047Reading (grade 7) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (grade 7) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading and may emphasize the use of language for different effects, in different contexts, and for different purposes. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 7.
0104801048Reading (grade 8)ReadingReading (grade 8) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading. Typically, these courses use various genres of literature to improve reading skills, and link exercises for different purposes to those reading selections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 8.
E0104801048Reading (grade 8) - English LearnerReadingSpecific to English Learners: Reading (grade 8) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading. Typically, these courses use various genres of literature to improve reading skills, and link exercises for different purposes to those reading selections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 8.
H0104801048Reading (grade 8) - Hearing ImpairedReadingSpecific to Hearing Impaired: Reading (grade 8) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading. Typically, these courses use various genres of literature to improve reading skills, and link exercises for different purposes to those reading selections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 8.
S0104801048Reading (grade 8) - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading (grade 8) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading. Typically, these courses use various genres of literature to improve reading skills, and link exercises for different purposes to those reading selections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 8.
V0104801048Reading (grade 8) - Visually ImpairedReadingSpecific to Visually Impaired: Reading (grade 8) courses build upon students’ prior knowledge and skill in reading. Typically, these courses use various genres of literature to improve reading skills, and link exercises for different purposes to those reading selections. Specific content depends upon state standards for grade 8.
0104901049Reading—GeneralReadingReading—General courses involve content that is not grade differentiated and may apply to a range of consecutive grades (e.g., "by third grade, students should know and be able to do…"). These courses include content that may be applicable to states or localities that do not employ grade-level content standards. [These courses provide broad content that is not organized as described elsewhere (i.e., Reading, early childhood education through grade 8.]
S0104901049Reading—General - Special EducationReadingSpecific to Special Education: Reading—General courses involve content that is not grade differentiated and may apply to a range of consecutive grades (e.g., "by third grade, students should know and be able to do…"). These courses include content that may be applicable to states or localities that do not employ grade-level content standards. [These courses provide broad content that is not organized as described elsewhere (i.e., Reading, early childhood education through grade 8.]
0105101051English/Literature (freshmen and sophomores)English/Language ArtsEnglish/Literature (freshmen and sophomores) courses are designed for freshmen and/or sophomores and typically introduce them to two or more genres of literature (novel, short story, poetry, and so on). Exploration of each genre’s literary elements; determination of theme and intent; and examination of vocabulary and semantics are often included in the course content. Writing assignments are required as an additional method to improve understanding and comprehension.
0105201052English/Literature (juniors and seniors)English/Language ArtsEnglish/Literature (juniors and seniors) courses are designed for juniors and/or seniors and emphasize comprehension, discernment, and critical-thinking skills in the reading of texts and literature. These courses introduce and explore more advanced literary techniques (irony, satire, humor, connotation, tone, rhythm, symbolism, and so on) through two or more literary genres, with the aim of creating sophisticated readers. Writing assignments are required as an additional method to develop and improve critical-thinking and analytic skills.
0105301053LiteratureEnglish/Language ArtsLiterature courses offer the opportunity for students to study and reflect upon the themes presented in the body of literature being presented. Students improve their critical-thinking skills as they determine the underlying assumptions and values within the reading selection and as they understand how the work reflects society’s problems and culture. Oral discussion is an integral part of literature courses, and written compositions are often required. Literature courses may survey representative works, reflect a particular genre or a specific theme, or survey works of a particular time or people.
0105401054American LiteratureEnglish/Language ArtsAmerican Literature courses focus upon commonly known American authors and their work. Students improve their critical-thinking skills as they determine the underlying assumptions and values within the selected works and as they understand how the literature reflects the society of the time. Oral discussion is an integral part of literature courses, and written compositions are often required.
0105501055American Literature/HistoryEnglish/Language ArtsAmerican Literature/History courses integrate the study of American literature with an overview of U.S. history. These courses may also include other aspects of American culture, such as art or music. A two-year sequence or two-period per day class may be required to cover the same objectives as would be covered separately in U.S. History Overview and American Literature.
0105601056British LiteratureEnglish/Language ArtsBritish Literature courses may provide a survey of British literature or may focus on a selected timeframe of England’s history. Students improve their critical-thinking skills as they determine the underlying assumptions and values within the selected works and as they understand how the literature reflects the society of the time. Oral discussion is an integral part of literature courses, and written compositions are often required.
0105701057British Literature/HistoryEnglish/Language ArtsBritish Literature/History courses integrate the study of British literature with an overview of the history of England. These courses may also include other aspects of British culture, such as art or music. A two-year sequence or two-period per day class may be required to cover the same objectives as would be covered separately in English History Overview and British Literature.
0105801058World LiteratureEnglish/Language ArtsWorld Literature courses use representative literature selections from ancient and/or modern times from countries around the world. Students improve their critical-thinking skills as they comprehend the diversity of literary traditions and the influences of those traditions. Oral discussion is an integral part of literature courses, and written compositions are often required.
0105901059Biblical LiteratureEnglish/Language ArtsBiblical Literature courses have the same aim as general literature courses (to improve students’ language arts and critical-thinking skills), focusing on the books of the Bible. Students may compare techniques, styles, and themes of the various books; examine the Bible’s influence on secular literature; and may study historical events of Biblical times. Oral discussion is an integral part of these courses, and written compositions are often required.
0106001060Literature of an AuthorEnglish/Language ArtsThese courses have the same aim as general literature courses (to improve students’ language arts and critical-thinking skills), focusing on a particular author and his or her work. Students determine the underlying assumptions and values within the selected works; compare techniques, styles, and themes of the author; and reflect upon the time period in which the author lived. Oral discussion is an integral part of literature courses, and written compositions are often required.
0106101061Literature of a GenreEnglish/Language ArtsThese courses have the same aim as general literature courses (to improve students’ language arts and critical-thinking skills), focusing on one or several genres, such as poetry, essay, biography, short story, drama, film, and so on. Students determine the underlying assumptions and values within the selected works and also examine the structure, techniques, and intentions of the genre being studied. Oral discussion is an integral part of these genre-oriented courses, and written compositions are often required.
0106201062Literature of a PeriodEnglish/Language ArtsThese courses have the same aim as general literature courses (to improve students’ language arts and critical-thinking skills), focusing on the literature written during or reflecting a particular time period (such as the French Revolution, the 1960s, or the 20th century). Students determine the underlying assumptions and values within the selected works, reflect upon the influence of societal events and social attitudes, and compare the points of view of various authors. Oral discussion is an integral part of literature courses, and written compositions are often required.
0106301063Literature of a PlaceEnglish/Language ArtsThese courses have the same aim as general literature courses (to improve students’ language arts and critical-thinking skills), focusing on a particular geographic region. Students determine the underlying assumptions and values within the selected works; study how the literature reflects the land, society, and history of the region; and may study the influence of this literature on others. Oral discussion is an integral part of literature courses, and written compositions are often required.
0106401064Literature of a PeopleEnglish/Language ArtsThese courses have the same aim as general literature courses (to improve students’ language arts and critical-thinking skills) but use literature written by authors who share a particular characteristic such as religion, culture, or gender. Students determine the underlying assumptions and values within the selected works, reflect upon the influence of a common characteristic, and compare the points of view of various authors. Oral discussion is an integral part of literature courses, and written compositions are often required.
0106501065Literature of a ThemeEnglish/Language ArtsThese courses have the same aim as general literature courses (to improve students’ language arts and critical-thinking skills) but use selected literature to explore a particular theme as expressed from several points of view. Such themes might include The American Dream, Society and Self, Exploration, War and Peace, and the like.
0106601066Strategic ReadingReadingStrategic Reading courses are intended to improve a student’s vocabulary, critical-thinking and analysis skills, or reading rate and comprehension level. Although these courses typically emphasize works of fiction, they may also include works of nonfiction (including textbooks). Strategic Reading courses often have a time-management focus, offering strategies for note-taking or for understanding and evaluating the important points of a text.
0106701067Assisted ReadingReadingAssisted Reading courses offer students the opportunity to focus on their reading skills. Assistance is targeted to students’ particular weaknesses and is designed to bring students’ reading comprehension up to the desired level or to develop strategies to read more efficiently.
0106801068Corrective ReadingReadingCorrective Reading courses offer diagnostic and remedial activities designed to correct reading difficulties and habits that interfere with students’ progress in developing reading skills and understandings. Activities are chosen to increase or improve students’ reading comprehension, reading technique, and general literacy skills.
0106901069MythologyEnglish/Language ArtsMythology courses introduce students to the origins and meaning of myths, including how cultures use myths to explain natural or social phenomenon. Students will explore the major themes, stories, characters, and archetypes present in the mythologies of several different cultures.
0107001070CI English LiteratureEnglish/Language ArtsCambridge International (CI) English Literature courses prepare students to take the Cambridge English Literature assessments. These courses teach students the three main forms of literature; prose, poetry, and drama, through texts from a wide range of different periods and cultures. Students will learn to read, analyze, and discuss the critical context of literature as well as develop skills of effective and appropriate communication.
0109701097Literature—Independent StudyEnglish/Language ArtsCourses in Literature—Independent Study, often conducted with instructors as mentors, enable students to explore topics of interest related to literature. Independent Study courses may serve as an opportunity for students to expand their expertise in a particular application, to explore a topic in greater detail, or to develop more advanced skills.
0109801098Literature—Workplace ExperienceEnglish/Language ArtsLiterature—Workplace Experience courses provide work experience in a field related to English literature. Goals are typically set cooperatively by the student, teacher, and employer (although students are not necessarily paid). These courses may include classroom activities as well, involving further study of the field or discussion regarding experiences that students encounter in the workplace.
0109901099Literature—OtherEnglish/Language ArtsOther Literature courses.
0110101101English/Composition (freshmen and sophomores)English/Language ArtsEnglish/Composition (freshmen and sophomores) courses are designed for freshmen and/or sophomores and build upon previous writing skills. These courses seek to develop the writing processes and practices necessary for producing successful high school compositions. Students typically learn to write persuasive, critical, and creative multiparagraph essays and compositions. While emphasizing composition, these courses may also incorporate some literature study to expose students to exemplary illustrations of various forms of writing.
0110201102English/Composition (juniors and seniors)English/Language ArtsEnglish/Composition (juniors and seniors) courses are designed for juniors and/or seniors and build upon previous writing skills. Reinforcing the logic and critical-thinking skills that accompany good writing, these courses—which emphasize word choice, usage, and writing mechanics—provide continued and advanced instruction in writing for a variety of purposes and audiences. English/Composition (juniors and seniors) courses may emphasize college or business preparation; literature study may be offered as an additional component in which students analyze examples of several genres.
0110301103CompositionWritingComposition courses focus on students’ writing skills and develop their ability to compose different types of papers for a range of purposes and audiences. These courses enable students to explore and practice descriptive, narrative, persuasive, or expositive styles as they write paragraphs, essays, letters, applications, formal documented papers, or technical reports. Although composition courses may present some opportunities for creative writing, their focus usually remains on nonfiction, scholarly, or formal writing.
0110401104Creative WritingWritingCreative Writing courses offer students the opportunity to develop and improve their technique and individual style in poetry, short story, drama, essays, and other forms of prose. The emphasis of the courses is on writing; however, students may study exemplary representations and authors to obtain a fuller appreciation of the form and craft. Although most creative writing classes cover several expressive forms, others concentrate exclusively on one particular form (such as poetry or playwriting).
